search_queries_used: site:edf.one chronic pruritus guideline anogenital history examination 2025
site:pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov European guideline chronic pruritus anogenital examination
candidate_sources_rejected: haemorrhoid guidance used to diagnose the cause of itching
automatic antifungal, steroid, hygiene, dietary, or anorectal procedure advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Anal itching has dermatologic, anorectal, infectious, irritant, and systemic causes.
Patient pages were not used as primary clinical evidence.
No treatment or procedure is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with chronic localised pruritus; children, pregnancy, immunocompromise, infection, inflammatory bowel disease, malignancy, and acute anorectal pain require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact chronic-pruritus section supports onset, duration, localisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, and clinician-performed anogenital examination without assigning a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, localisation, generalisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, whole skin, scalp, nails, oral and anogenital examination, and primary versus secondary lesions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diagnostic-work-up section supports recording already available results and clinician-selected investigation context without generating a test panel.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording laboratory or other results already available and clinician-selected investigation context according to history and examination; it does not generate a test panel.

unresolved_source_gaps: Specific anorectal symptoms, sexual or safeguarding context, consent, chaperone, examination, infection, cause, test, treatment, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
